title: Hardware vs. Software Reliability in MCU-Based Control Systems
summary: A compact engineering comparison of hardware and software reliability in MCU-based control systems, using FIT, MTTF, ISO 13849-1, SN 29500, and semiconductor vendor data.

title: The Embedded Software Development Processes syllabus for KPI: why I created it
summary: Why I created the Embedded Software Development Processes syllabus for KPI: expected background, hands-on structure, toolchain, and evaluation logic.

title: ISE 2026, Barcelona: brief travel notes
summary: Short notes from ISE 2026: event format, Kyiv–Krakow–Barcelona travel logistics, city transport, food, weather/clothing, and a few useful contacts.

title: KNUBA KBKI research seminar: interim PhD review and transition plan toward the external seminar and pre-defenses
summary: On 24 December 2025, the KBKI department at KNUBA held a 30+30 research seminar. The goal was a technical readiness review before the external seminar and pre-defenses, with critical corrections captured inside a managed quality cycle.

title: Exhibitions as a tool for winning customers and partners: Embedded World 2023 and The Battery Show 2023
summary: A short practical memo from Embedded World 2023 and The Battery Show 2023: how to prepare, who to bring, and how to convert contacts into partnerships.

title: KNUBA master's pre-defense (KBKI), December 2025: outcomes and observations
summary: From 15 to 18 December 2025, I participated in the master's pre-defense at the KBKI department of KNUBA as Deputy Chair of the State Examination Board. Roughly 85% of students were recommended for defense; the rest were not recommended after a collegial review.

title: MATLAB EXPO Deutschland 2025: key themes and takeaways
summary: Notes from MATLAB EXPO Deutschland 2025: edge AI, model-based engineering, FuSa/traceability, PIL/HIL, and large-scale energy use cases such as HVDC.

title: N-iX × KPI: a memorandum that actually works
summary: How the N-iX × KPI memorandum turned into regular work: weekly coordination, joint R&D topics, and the idea of a new academic program at the intersection of business and science.

title: Auto.AI Europe 2024: a tight-knit community and practical takeaways
summary: Notes from Auto.AI Europe (09.2024, Berlin): why it matters to belong to the community, what I moderated in the energy-efficiency track, and what practical outcome followed after the trip.
